steps.description: Press the "F1" key to open the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code workspace interface.
Zoom out the interface by pressing "Ctrl" and "-" keys in Visual Studio Code while the command palette is active.
Type the text 'Welcome: Open Walkthrough' into the Command Palette search bar in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Welcome: Open Walkthrough…" option in the command palette of Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Build a Declarative Agent" option in the walkthrough dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion There is content under 'Introduction', it is not empty.
Click the "Set up your environment" radio button within the "Build a Declarative Agent" tutorial interface in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion 'Check Copilot License' button is visible
Click the hyperlink labeled "Learn how to set up a developer environment" under the "Set up your environment" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Refresh the webpage displaying "Set up your development environment for Microsoft 365 Copilot" within the Microsoft Learn platform by pressing the F5 key.
Refresh the browser displaying the "Set up your development environment for Microsoft 365 Copilot" page by simulating an F5 key press.
Refresh the web page displaying the "Set up your development environment for Microsoft 365 Copilot" article under the Microsoft Learn platform by pressing the F5 key.
@assertion The page url is "https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365-copilot/extensibility/prerequisites#prerequisites", and not returns error response.
Click the browser's "Close tab" button in the top-left corner of the window at coordinates (261, 27).
Click the "Build Declarative Agent"" button located under the "Set up your environment" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit Welcome interface.
@assertion 'Build Declarative Agent' button is visible.
Click the "Preview in Microsoft 365 Copilot" button within the "Build a declarative agent" section on the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit Welcome interface.
@assertion 'Provision' button and 'Preview' button is visible
@assertion 'Provision' link and 'Preview' link and 'Run and Debug' link is visible
Click the checkbox for "Improve your declarative agent" under the "Preview in Microsoft 365 Copilot" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
@assertion There is content under 'Improve your declarative agent' section, it is not empty.
Click the "Resources" option within the guided tutorial panel under "Improve your declarative agent."
Click the "Generative AI for Beginners" link under the "Resources" section in the "Build a Declarative Agent" page within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Refresh the webpage in a Chrome browser displaying an "Aw, Snap!" error by pressing the F5 key.
Refresh the GitHub repository page displaying the "microsoft/generative-ai-for-beginners" project using the F5 key.
Refresh the GitHub repository page (URL: github.com/microsoft/generative-ai-for-beginners/tree/main) by pressing the F5 key.
@assertion The page url is "https://github.com/microsoft/generative-ai-for-beginners/tree/main", and not returns error response.
Click the browser's "Close" button at the top-right corner of the application window to exit the current GitHub page.
Click the "Retrieval Augmented Generation" link in the "Resources" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome interface.
Refresh the "Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)" article page in Microsoft Learn using the F5 key.
Refresh the webpage displaying "Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) in Azure AI Search" by pressing the F5 key.
Refresh the Microsoft Learn page displaying 'Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) in Azure AI Search' by pressing the F5 key.
@assertion The page url is "https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/retrieval-augmented-generation-overview?tabs=docs", and not returns error response.
Click the "X" button to close the banner indicating "Google Chrome isn't your default browser" at the top-right corner of the application window.
Click the "AI Learning and Community Hub" hyperlink under the "Resources" section within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Refresh the browser page in Google Chrome using the 'F5' key, while on the Microsoft Learn - AI learning hub webpage.
Refresh the "AI learning hub" page in Google Chrome by pressing F5.
Refresh the webpage in the Google Chrome browser on the "AI learning hub" page.
@assertion The page url is "https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/ai/?tabs=developer", and not returns error response.
